sentences.text: This chapter describes the RISC-V hypervisor extension, which virtualizes the supervisor-level architecture to support the efficient hosting of guest operating systems atop a type-1 or type-2 hypervisor.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension changes supervisor mode into hypervisor-extended supervisor mode (HS-mode, or hypervisor mode for short), where a hypervisor or a hosting-capable operating system runs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension also adds another stage of address translation, from guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses, to virtualize the memory and memory-mapped I/O subsystems for a guest operating system. HS-mode acts the same as S-mode, but with additional instructions and CSRs that control the new stage of address translation and support hosting a guest OS in virtual S-mode (VS-mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: Regular S-mode operating systems can execute without modification either in HS-mode or as VS-mode guests.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: In HS-mode, an OS or hypervisor interacts with the machine through the same SBI as an OS normally does from S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: An HS-mode hypervisor is expected to implement the SBI for its VS-mode guest.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension depends on an "I" base integer ISA with 32 `x` registers (RV32I or RV64I), not RV32E or RV64E, which have only 16 `x` registers. CSR `mtval` must not be read-only zero, and standard page-based address translation must be supported, either Sv32 for RV32, or a minimum of Sv39 for RV64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension is enabled by setting bit 7 in the `misa` CSR, which corresponds to the letter H. RISC-V harts that implement the hypervisor extension are encouraged not to hardwire `misa`[7], so that the extension may be disabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The current virtualization mode, denoted V, indicates whether the hart is currently executing in a guest.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: When V=1, the hart is either in virtual S-mode (VS-mode), or in virtual U-mode (VU-mode) atop a guest OS running in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: When V=0, the hart is either in M-mode, in HS-mode, or in U-mode atop an OS running in HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: The virtualization mode also indicates whether two-stage address translation is active (V=1) or inactive (V=0). lists the possible privilege modes of a RISC-V hart with the hypervisor extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: .Privilege modes with the hypervisor extension. [float="center",align="center",cols="~,~,~,~,~"] | ^|Virtualization + Mode (V) ^|Nominal Privilege |Abbreviation |Name |Two-Stage Translation
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: ^|0 + 0 + 0 ^| U + S + M |U-mode + HS-mode + M-mode |User mode + Hypervisor-extended supervisor mode + Machine mode |Off + Off + Off ^|1 + 1 ^|U + S |VU-mode + VS-mode |Virtual user mode + Virtual supervisor mode |On + On |
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: For privilege modes U and VU, the nominal privilege mode is U, and for privilege modes HS and VS, the nominal privilege mode is S.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: HS-mode is more privileged than VS-mode, and VS-mode is more privileged than VU-mode. VS-mode interrupts are globally disabled when executing in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Privilege Modes

sentences.text: An OS or hypervisor running in HS-mode uses the supervisor CSRs to interact with the exception, interrupt, and address-translation subsystems.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Additional CSRs are provided to HS-mode, but not to VS-mode, to manage two-stage address translation and to control the behavior of a VS-mode guest: `hstatus`, `hedeleg`, `hideleg`, `hvip`, `hip`, `hie`, `hgeip`, `hgeie`, `henvcfg`, `henvcfgh`, `hcounteren`, `htimedelta`, `htimedeltah`, `htval`, `htinst`, and `hgatp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Furthermore, several virtual supervisor CSRs (VS CSRs) are replicas of the normal supervisor CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: For example, `vsstatus` is the VS CSR that duplicates the usual `sstatus` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: When V=1, the VS CSRs substitute for the corresponding supervisor CSRs, taking over all functions of the usual supervisor CSRs except as specified otherwise.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Instructions that normally read or modify a supervisor CSR shall instead access the corresponding VS CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: When V=1, an attempt to read or write a VS CSR directly by its own separate CSR address causes a virtual-instruction exception. (Attempts from U-mode cause an illegal-instruction exception as usual.) The VS CSRs can be accessed as themselves only from M-mode or HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: While V=1, the normal HS-level supervisor CSRs that are replaced by VS CSRs retain their values but do not affect the behavior of the machine unless specifically documented to do so.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Conversely, when V=0, the VS CSRs do not ordinarily affect the behavior of the machine other than being readable and writable by CSR instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Some standard supervisor CSRs (`senvcfg`, `scounteren`, and `scontext`, possibly others) have no matching VS CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: These supervisor CSRs continue to have their usual function and accessibility even when V=1, except with VS-mode and VU-mode substituting for HS-mode and U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: Hypervisor software is expected to manually swap the contents of these registers as needed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: In this chapter, we use the term HSXLEN to refer to the effective XLEN when executing in HS-mode, and VSXLEN to refer to the effective XLEN when executing in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs

sentences.text: The `hstatus` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in when HSXLEN=32 and when HSXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `hstatus` register provides facilities analogous to the `mstatus` register for tracking and controlling the exception behavior of a VS-mode guest.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor status register (`hstatus`) when HSXLEN=32 [wavedrom,, svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 5, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VSBE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'GVA'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPV'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPVP'}, {bits: 1, name: 'HU'}, {bits: 2, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 6, name: 'VGEIN'}, {bits: 2, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTVM'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTW'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTSR'}, {bits: 9, name: 'WPRI'}, ], config:{lanes: 2, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor status register (`hstatus`) when HSXLEN=64. [wavedrom,, svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 5, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VSBE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'GVA'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPV'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPVP'}, {bits: 1, name: 'HU'}, {bits: 2, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 6, name: 'VGEIN'}, {bits: 2, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTVM'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTW'}, {bits: 1, name: 'VTSR'}, {bits: 9, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 2, name: 'VSXL'}, {bits: 14, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 2, name: 'HUPMM'}, {bits: 14, name: 'WPRI'}, ], config:{lanes: 4, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The VSXL field controls the effective XLEN for VS-mode (known as VSXLEN), which may differ from the XLEN for HS-mode (HSXLEN).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When HSXLEN=32, the VSXL field does not exist, and VSXLEN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When HSXLEN=64, VSXL is a *WARL* field that is encoded the same as the MXL field of `misa`, shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: In particular, an implementation may make VSXL be a read-only field whose value always ensures that VSXLEN=HSXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: If HSXLEN is changed from 32 to a wider width, and if field VSXL is not restricted to a single value, it gets the value corresponding to the widest supported width not wider than the new HSXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `hstatus` fields VTSR, VTW, and VTVM are defined analogously to the `mstatus` fields TSR, TW, and TVM, but affect execution only in VS-mode, and cause virtual-instruction exceptions instead of illegal-instruction exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VTSR=1, an attempt in VS-mode to execute SRET raises a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VTW=1 (and assuming `mstatus`.TW=0), an attempt in VS-mode to execute WFI raises a virtual-instruction exception if the WFI does not complete within an implementation-specific, bounded time limit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: An implementation may have WFI always raise a virtual-instruction exception in VS-mode when VTW=1 (and `mstatus`.TW=0), even if there are pending globally-disabled interrupts when the instruction is executed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VTVM=1, an attempt in VS-mode to execute SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA or to access CSR `satp` raises a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The VGEIN (Virtual Guest External Interrupt Number) field selects a guest external interrupt source for VS-level external interrupts. VGEIN is a *WLRL* field that must be able to hold values between zero and the maximum guest external interrupt number (known as GEILEN), inclusive.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VGEIN=0, no guest external interrupt source is selected for VS-level external interrupts. GEILEN may be zero, in which case VGEIN may be read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Guest external interrupts are explained in , and the use of VGEIN is covered further in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Field HU (Hypervisor in U-mode) controls whether the virtual-machine load/store instructions, HLV, HLVX, and HSV, can be used also in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When HU=1, these instructions can be executed in U-mode the same as in HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When HU=0, all hypervisor instructions cause an illegal-instruction exception in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When Ssnpm extension is implemented, the `HUPMM` field enables or disables pointer masking (see ) for `HLV.\*` and `HSV.*` instructions in U-mode, according to the values in , when their explicit memory access is performed as though in VU-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: In HS- and M-modes, pointer masking for these instructions is enabled or disabled by `senvcfg.PMM`, when their explicit memory access is performed as though in VU-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Setting `henvcfg.PMM` enables or disables pointer masking for `HLV.\*` and `HSV.*` when their explicit memory access is performed as though in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When the Ssnpm extension is not implemented, the `HUPMM` field is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `HUPMM` field is read-only zero for RV32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The SPV bit (Supervisor Previous Virtualization mode) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Just as the SPP bit in `sstatus` is set to the (nominal) privilege mode at the time of the trap, the SPV bit in `hstatus` is set to the value of the virtualization mode V at the time of the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When an SRET instruction is executed when V=0, V is set to SPV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When V=1 and a trap is taken into HS-mode, bit SPVP (Supervisor Previous Virtual Privilege) is set to the nominal privilege mode at the time of the trap, the same as `sstatus`.SPP.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: But if V=0 before a trap, SPVP is left unchanged on trap entry. SPVP controls the effective privilege of explicit memory accesses made by the virtual-machine load/store instructions, HLV, HLVX, and HSV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Field GVA (Guest Virtual Address) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: For any trap (breakpoint, address misaligned, access fault, page fault, or guest-page fault) that writes a guest virtual address to `stval`, GVA is set to 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: For any other trap into HS-mode, GVA is set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The VSBE bit is a *WARL* field that controls the endianness of explicit memory accesses made from VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: If VSBE=0, explicit load and store memory accesses made from VS-mode are little-endian, and if VSBE=1, they are big-endian. VSBE also controls the endianness of all implicit accesses to VS-level memory management data structures, such as page tables.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: An implementation may make VSBE a read-only field that always specifies the same endianness as HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Status (`hstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Register `hedeleg` is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Register `hideleg` is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: By default, all traps at any privilege level are handled in M-mode, though M-mode usually uses the `medeleg` and `mideleg` CSRs to delegate some traps to HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The `hedeleg` and `hideleg` CSRs allow these traps to be further delegated to a VS-mode guest; their layout is the same as `medeleg` and `mideleg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: .Hypervisor exception delegation register (`hedeleg`). include::images/bytefield/hedelegreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: .Hypervisor interrupt delegation register (`hideleg`). include::images/bytefield/hidelegreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: A synchronous trap that has been delegated to HS-mode (using `medeleg`) is further delegated to VS-mode if V=1 before the trap and the corresponding `hedeleg` bit is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Each bit of `hedeleg` shall be either writable or read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Many bits of `hedeleg` are required specifically to be writable or zero, as enumerated in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Bit 0, corresponding to instruction address-misaligned exceptions, must be writable if IALIGN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, `hedelegh` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `hedeleg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Register `hedelegh` does not exist when XLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: An interrupt that has been delegated to HS-mode (using `mideleg`) is further delegated to VS-mode if the corresponding `hideleg` bit is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Among bits 15:0 of `hideleg`, bits 10, 6, and 2 (corresponding to the standard VS-level interrupts) are writable, and bits 12, 9, 5, and 1 (corresponding to the standard S-level interrupts) are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: When a virtual supervisor external interrupt (code 10) is delegated to VS-mode, it is automatically translated by the machine into a supervisor external interrupt (code 9) for VS-mode, including the value written to `vscause` on an interrupt trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Likewise, a virtual supervisor timer interrupt (6) is translated into a supervisor timer interrupt (5) for VS-mode, and a virtual supervisor software interrupt (2) is translated into a supervisor software interrupt (1) for VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Similar translations may or may not be done for platform interrupt causes (codes 16 and above).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Delegation (`hedeleg` and `hideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Register `hvip` is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register that a hypervisor can write to indicate virtual interrupts intended for VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits of `hvip` that are not writable are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Hypervisor virtual-interrupt-pending register(`hvip`). include::images/bytefield/hvipreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The standard portion (bits 15:0) of `hvip` is formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits VSEIP, VSTIP, and VSSIP of `hvip` are writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Setting VSEIP=1 in `hvip` asserts a VS-level external interrupt; setting VSTIP asserts a VS-level timer interrupt; and setting VSSIP asserts a VS-level software interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `hvip`. include::images/bytefield/hvipreg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Registers `hip` and `hie` are HSXLEN-bit read/write registers that supplement HS-level’s `sip` and `sie` respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `hip` register indicates pending VS-level and hypervisor-specific interrupts, while `hie` contains enable bits for the same interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Hypervisor interrupt-pending register (`hip`). include::images/bytefield/hipreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Hypervisor interrupt-enable register (`hie`). include::images/bytefield/hiereg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: For each writable bit in `sie`, the corresponding bit shall be read-only zero in both `hip` and `hie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Hence, the nonzero bits in `sie` and `hie` are always mutually exclusive, and likewise for `sip` and `hip`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: An interrupt i will trap to HS-mode whenever all of the following are true: (a) either the current operating mode is HS-mode and the SIE bit in the `sstatus` register is set, or the current operating mode has less privilege than HS-mode; (b) bit i is set in both `sip` and `sie`, or in both `hip` and `hie`; and (c) bit i is not set in `hideleg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: If bit i of `sie` is read-only zero, the same bit in register `hip` may be writable or may be read-only.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit i in `hip` is writable, a pending interrupt i can be cleared by writing 0 to this bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: If interrupt i can become pending in `hip` but bit i in `hip` is read-only, then either the interrupt can be cleared by clearing bit i of `hvip`, or the implementation must provide some other mechanism for clearing the pending interrupt (which may involve a call to the execution environment).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: A bit in `hie` shall be writable if the corresponding interrupt can ever become pending in `hip`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits of `hie` that are not writable shall be read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The standard portions (bits 15:0) of registers `hip` and `hie` are formatted as shown in and respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `hip`. include::images/bytefield/hipreg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `hie`. include::images/bytefield/hiereg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `hip`.SGEIP and `hie`.SGEIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for guest external interrupts at supervisor level (HS-level). SGEIP is read-only in `hip`, and is 1 if and only if the bitwise logical-AND of CSRs `hgeip` and `hgeie` is nonzero in any bit. (See .)
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `hip`.VSEIP and `hie`.VSEIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for VS-level external interrupts. VSEIP is read-only in `hip`, and is the logical-OR of these interrupt sources:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: bit VSEIP of `hvip`; * the bit of `hgeip` selected by `hstatus`.VGEIN; and * any other platform-specific external interrupt signal directed to VS-level.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `hip`.VSTIP and `hie`.VSTIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for VS-level timer interrupts. VSTIP is read-only in `hip`, and is the logical-OR of `hvip`.VSTIP and, when the Sstc extension is implemented, the timer interrupt signal resulting from `vstimecmp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `hip`.VSTIP bit, in response to timer interrupts generated by `vstimecmp`, is set by writing `vstimecmp` with a value that is less than or equal to the sum of `time` and `htimedelta`, truncated to 64 bits; it is cleared by writing `vstimecmp` with a greater value.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `hip`.VSTIP bit remains defined while V=0 as well as V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `hip`.VSSIP and `hie`.VSSIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for VS-level software interrupts. VSSIP in `hip` is an alias (writable) of the same bit in `hvip`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: Multiple simultaneous interrupts destined for HS-mode are handled in the following decreasing priority order: SEI, SSI, STI, SGEI, VSEI, VSSI, VSTI, LCOFI.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Interrupt (`hvip`, `hip`, and `hie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `hgeip` register is an HSXLEN-bit read-only register, formatted as shown in , that indicates pending guest external interrupts for this hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: The `hgeie` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in , that contains enable bits for the guest external interrupts at this hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: Guest external interrupt number i corresponds with bit i in both `hgeip` and `hgeie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: .Hypervisor guest external interrupt-pending register (`hgeip`). include::images/bytefield/hgeipreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: .Hypervisor guest external interrupt-enable register (`hgeie`). include::images/bytefield/hgeiereg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: Guest external interrupts represent interrupts directed to individual virtual machines at VS-level.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: If a RISC-V platform supports placing a physical device under the direct control of a guest OS with minimal hypervisor intervention (known as pass-through or direct assignment between a virtual machine and the physical device), then, in such circumstance, interrupts from the device are intended for a specific virtual machine.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: Each bit of `hgeip` summarizes all pending interrupts directed to one virtual hart, as collected and reported by an interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: To distinguish specific pending interrupts from multiple devices, software must query the interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: The number of bits implemented in `hgeip` and `hgeie` for guest external interrupts is UNSPECIFIED and may be zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: This number is known as GEILEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: The least-significant bits are implemented first, apart from bit 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: Hence, if GEILEN is nonzero, bits GEILEN:1 shall be writable in `hgeie`, and all other bit positions shall be read-only zeros in both `hgeip` and `hgeie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: Register `hgeie` selects the subset of guest external interrupts that cause a supervisor-level (HS-level) guest external interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: The enable bits in `hgeie` do not affect the VS-level external interrupt signal selected from `hgeip` by `hstatus`.VGEIN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest External Interrupt Registers (`hgeip` and `hgeie`)

sentences.text: The `henvcfg` CSR is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in , that controls certain characteristics of the execution environment when virtualization mode V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: .Hypervisor environment configuration register (`henvcfg`). [wavedrom, ,svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 1, name: 'FIOM'}, {bits: 1, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'LPE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SSE'}, {bits: 2, name: 'CBIE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'CBCFE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'CBZE'}, {bits: 24, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 2, name: 'PMM'}, {bits: 25, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'DTE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'ADUE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'PBMTE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'STCE'}, ], config:{lanes: 4, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If bit FIOM (Fence of I/O implies Memory) is set to one in `henvcfg`, FENCE instructions executed when V=1 are modified so the requirement to order accesses to device I/O implies also the requirement to order main memory accesses. details the modified interpretation of FENCE instruction bits PI, PO, SI, and SO when FIOM=1 and V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: Similarly, when FIOM=1 and V=1, if an atomic instruction that accesses a region ordered as device I/O has its aq and/or rl bit set, then that instruction is ordered as though it accesses both device I/O and memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: .Modified interpretation of FENCE predecessor and successor sets when FIOM=1 and virtualization mode V=1. | |Instruction bit |Meaning when set |PI + PO |Predecessor device input and memory reads (PR implied) + Predecessor device output and memory writes (PW implied) |SI + SO |Successor device input and memory reads (SR implied) + Successor device output and memory writes (SW implied) |
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The PBMTE bit controls whether the Svpbmt extension is available for use in VS-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When PBMTE=1, Svpbmt is available for VS-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When PBMTE=0, the implementation behaves as though Svpbmt were not implemented for VS-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If Svpbmt is not implemented, PBMTE is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the Svadu extension is implemented, the ADUE bit controls whether hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled for VS-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When ADUE=1, hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled during VS-stage address translation, and the implementation behaves as though the Svade extension were not implemented for VS-mode address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When ADUE=0, the implementation behaves as though Svade were implemented for VS-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If Svadu is not implemented, ADUE is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Sstc extension adds the `STCE` (STimecmp Enable) bit to `henvcfg` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the Sstc extension is not implemented, `STCE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The `STCE` bit enables `vstimecmp` for VS-mode when set to one.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When `STCE` bit is `henvcfg` is zero, an attempt to access `stimecmp` (really `vstimecmp`) when V=1 raises a virtual-instruction exception, and `VSTIP` in `hip` reverts to its defined behavior as if this extension is not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Zicboz extension adds the `CBZE` (Cache Block Zero instruction enable) field to `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The `CBZE` field applies to execution of the cache block zero instruction (`CBO.ZERO`) in privilege modes VS and VU, and only when the instruction is HS-qualified.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the instruction is not HS-qualified, it raises an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the instruction is HS-qualified and the `CBZE` field is set to 1, the instruction is enabled for execution; otherwise, if the `CBZE` field is set to 0, it raises a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the Zicboz extension is not implemented, `CBZE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Zicbom extension adds the `CBCFE` (Cache Block Clean and Flush instruction Enable) field to `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When V=1, if the `CBO.CLEAN` and `CBO.FLUSH` instructions are not HS-qualified, they raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the instructions are HS-qualified and the `CBCFE` field is set to 1, the instructions are enabled for execution; otherwise, if the `CBCFE` field is set to 0, they raise a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, `CBCFE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Zicbom extension adds the `CBIE` (Cache Block Invalidate instruction Enable) WARL field to `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The `CBIE` field controls execution of the cache block invalidate instruction (`CBO.INVAL`) in privilege modes VS and VU.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The encoding `10b` is reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, `CBIE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When V=1, if the `CBO.INVAL` instruction is not HS-qualified, it raises an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the instruction is HS-qualified and the `CBIE` field is set to `01b` or `11b`, the instruction is enabled for execution; otherwise, it raises a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If `CBO.INVAL` is enabled in HS-mode to perform a flush operation, then when the instruction is enabled in VS- or VU-mode it performs a flush operation, even if `CBIE` is set to `11b`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: Otherwise, when the instruction is enabled for execution, its behavior depends on the `CBIE` encoding, as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: `01b` -- The instruction is executed and performs a flush operation, even if configured by VS-mode to perform an invalidate operation. * `11b` -- The instruction is executed and performs an invalidate operation, unless configured by VS-mode to perform a flush operation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the Ssnpm extension is implemented, the `PMM` field enables or disables pointer masking (see ) for VS-mode, according to the values in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the Ssnpm extension is not implemented, the `PMM` field is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The `PMM` field is read-only zero for RV32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: .Legal values of `PMM` WARL field | |Value|Description |00|Pointer masking is disabled (PMLEN = 0) |01|Reserved |10|Pointer masking is enabled with PMLEN = XLEN - 57 (PMLEN = 7 on RV64) |11|Pointer masking is enabled with PMLEN = XLEN - 48 (PMLEN = 16 on RV64) |
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `LPE` field in `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the `LPE` field is set to 1, the Zicfilp extension is enabled in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the `LPE` field is 0, the Zicfilp extension is not enabled in VS-mode and the following rules apply to VS-mode:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The hart does not update the `ELP` state; it remains as `NOLPEXPECTED`. * The `LPAD` instruction operates as a no-op.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Zicfiss extension adds the `SSE` field in `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: If the `SSE` field is set to 1, the Zicfiss extension is activated in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When the `SSE` field is 0, the Zicfiss extension remains inactive in VS-mode, and the following rules apply when `V=1`:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: 32-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zimop. * 16-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zcmop. * The `pte.xwr=010b` encoding in VS-stage page tables becomes reserved. * The `senvcfg.SSE` field will read as zero and is read-only. *
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When `menvcfg.SSE` is one, `SSAMOSWAP.W/D` raises a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The Ssdbltrp extension adds the double-trap-enable (`DTE`) field in `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When `henvcfg.DTE` is zero, the implementation behaves as though Ssdbltrp is not implemented for VS-mode and the `vsstatus.SDT` bit is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, `henvcfgh` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `henvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: Register `henvcfgh` does not exist when XLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

sentences.text: The counter-enable register `hcounteren` is a 32-bit register that controls the availability of the hardware performance monitoring counters to the guest virtual machine.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor counter-enable register (`hcounteren`). include::images/bytefield/hcounterenreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When the CY, TM, IR, or HPMn bit in the `hcounteren` register is clear, attempts to read the `cycle`, `time`, `instret`, or `hpmcounter` n register while V=1 will cause a virtual-instruction exception if the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When one of these bits is set, access to the corresponding register is permitted when V=1, unless prevented for some other reason.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: In VU-mode, a counter is not readable unless the applicable bits are set in both `hcounteren` and `scounteren`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: In addition, when the TM bit in the `hcounteren` register is clear, attempts to access the `vstimecmp` register (via `stimecmp`) while executing in VS-mode will cause a virtual-instruction exception if the same bit in `mcounteren` is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When this bit and the same bit in `mcounteren` are both set, access to the `vstimecmp` register (if implemented) is permitted in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: `hcounteren` must be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: However, any of the bits may be read-only zero, indicating reads to the corresponding counter will cause an exception when V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: Hence, they are effectively *WARL* fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The `htimedelta` CSR is a 64-bit read/write register that contains the delta between the value of the `time` CSR and the value returned in VS-mode or VU-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: That is, reading the `time` CSR in VS or VU mode returns the sum of the contents of `htimedelta` and the actual value of `time`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor time delta register. include::images/bytefield/htimedelta.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, `htimedeltah` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `htimedelta`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: Register `htimedeltah` does not exist when XLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: If the `time` CSR is implemented, `htimedelta` (and `htimedeltah` for XLEN=32) must be implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

sentences.text: The `htval` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into HS-mode, `htval` is written with additional exception-specific information, alongside `stval`, to assist software in handling the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor trap value register (`htval`). include::images/bytefield/htvalreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: When a guest-page-fault trap is taken into HS-mode, `htval` is written with either zero or the guest physical address that faulted, shifted right by 2 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: For other traps, `htval` is set to zero, but a future standard or extension may redefine `htval's` setting for other traps.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: A guest-page fault may arise due to an implicit memory access during first-stage (VS-stage) address translation, in which case a guest physical address written to `htval` is that of the implicit memory access that faulted—for example, the address of a VS-level page table entry that could not be read. (The guest physical address corresponding to the original virtual address is unknown when VS-stage translation fails to complete.) Additional information is provided in CSR `htinst` to disambiguate such situations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, for misaligned loads and stores that cause guest-page faults, a nonzero guest physical address in `htval` corresponds to the faulting portion of the access as indicated by the virtual address in `stval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: For instruction guest-page faults on systems with variable-length instructions, a nonzero `htval` corresponds to the faulting portion of the instruction as indicated by the virtual address in `stval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: `htval` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold zero and may be capable of holding only an arbitrary subset of other 2-bit-shifted guest physical addresses, if any.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

sentences.text: The `htinst` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into HS-mode, `htinst` is written with a value that, if nonzero, provides information about the instruction that trapped, to assist software in handling the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

sentences.text: The values that may be written to `htinst` on a trap are documented in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor trap instruction (`htinst`) register. include::images/bytefield/htinstreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

sentences.text: `htinst` is a *WARL* register that need only be able to hold the values that the implementation may automatically write to it on a trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

sentences.text: The `hgatp` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in for HSXLEN=32 and for HSXLEN=64, which controls G-stage address translation and protection, the second stage of two-stage translation for guest virtual addresses (see ).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: Similar to CSR `satp`, this register holds the physical page number (PPN) of the guest-physical root page table; a virtual machine identifier (VMID), which facilitates address-translation fences on a per-virtual-machine basis; and the MODE field, which selects the address-translation scheme for guest physical addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: When `mstatus`.TVM=1, attempts to read or write `hgatp` while executing in HS-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor guest address translation and protection register `hgatp` when HSXLEN=32. include::images/bytefield/rv32hgatp.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: .Hypervisor guest address translation and protection register `hgatp` when HSXLEN=64 for MODE values Bare, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4. include::images/bytefield/rv64hgatp.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: shows the encodings of the MODE field when HSXLEN=32 and HSXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: When MODE=Bare, guest physical addresses are equal to supervisor physical addresses, and there is no further memory protection for a guest virtual machine beyond the physical memory protection scheme described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: In this case, software must write zero to the remaining fields in `hgatp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: Attempting to select MODE=Bare with a nonzero pattern in the remaining fields has an UNSPECIFIED effect on the value that the remaining fields assume and an UNSPECIFIED effect on G-stage address translation and protection behavior.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: When HSXLEN=32, the only other valid setting for MODE is Sv32x4, which is a modification of the usual Sv32 paged virtual-memory scheme, extended to support 34-bit guest physical addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: When HSXLEN=64, modes Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4 are defined as modifications of the Sv39, Sv48, and Sv57 paged virtual-memory schemes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: All of these paged virtual-memory schemes are described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The remaining MODE settings when HSXLEN=64 are reserved for future use and may define different interpretations of the other fields in `hgatp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: .Encoding of `hgatp` MODE field. | 3+|HSXLEN=32 |Value |Name |Description |0 + 1 |Bare + Sv32x4 |No translation or protection. + Page-based 34-bit virtual addressing (2-bit extension of Sv32). 3+s|HSXLEN=64 |Value |Name |Description |0 + 1-7 + 8 + 9 + 10 + 11-15 |Bare + — + Sv39x4 + Sv48x4 + Sv57x4 + — |No translation or protection. + Reserved + Page-based 41-bit virtual addressing (2-bit extension of Sv39). + Page-based 50-bit virtual addressing (2-bit extension of Sv48). + Page-based 59-bit virtual addressing (2-bit extension of Sv57). + Reserved |
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: Implementations are not required to support all defined MODE settings when HSXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: A write to `hgatp` with an unsupported MODE value is not ignored as it is for `satp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: Instead, the fields of `hgatp` are *WARL* in the normal way, when so indicated.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: As explained in , for the paged virtual-memory schemes (Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4), the root page table is 16 KiB and must be aligned to a 16-KiB boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: In these modes, the lowest two bits of the physical page number (PPN) in `hgatp` always read as zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: An implementation that supports only the defined paged virtual-memory schemes and/or Bare may make PPN[1:0] read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The number of VMID bits is UNSPECIFIED and may be zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The number of implemented VMID bits, termed VMIDLEN, may be determined by writing one to every bit position in the VMID field, then reading back the value in `hgatp` to see which bit positions in the VMID field hold a one.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The least-significant bits of VMID are implemented first: that is, if VMIDLEN > 0, VMID[VMIDLEN-1:0] is writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The maximal value of VMIDLEN, termed VMIDMAX, is 7 for Sv32x4 or 14 for Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The `hgatp` register is considered active for the purposes of the address-translation algorithm unless the effective privilege mode is U and `hstatus`.HU=0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: Note that writing `hgatp` does not imply any ordering constraints between page-table updates and subsequent G-stage address translations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: If the new virtual machine’s guest physical page tables have been modified, or if a VMID is reused, it may be necessary to execute an HFENCE.GVMA instruction (see ) before or after writing `hgatp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

sentences.text: The `vsstatus` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `sstatus`, formatted as shown in when VSXLEN=32 and when VSXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When V=1, `vsstatus` substitutes for the usual `sstatus`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sstatus` actually access `vsstatus` instead.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register
