[wavedrom, ,svg] .... {reg: [ {bits: 1, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SIE'}, {bits: 3, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPIE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'UBE'}, {bits: 1, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPP'}, {bits: 2, name: 'VS[1:0]'}, {bits: 2, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 2, name: 'FS[1:0]'}, {bits: 2, name: 'XS[1:0]'}, {bits: 1, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SUM'}, {bits: 1, name: 'MXR'}, {bits: 3, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SPELP'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SDT'}, {bits: 7, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 2, name: 'UXL[1:0]'}, {bits: 29, name: 'WPRI'}, {bits: 1, name: 'SD'}, ], config:{lanes: 4, hspace:1024}} ....
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The UXL field controls the effective XLEN for VU-mode, which may differ from the XLEN for VS-mode (VSXLEN).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VSXLEN=32, the UXL field does not exist, and VU-mode XLEN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When VSXLEN=64, UXL is a *WARL* field that is encoded the same as the MXL field of `misa`, shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: In particular, an implementation may make UXL be a read-only copy of field VSXL of `hstatus`, forcing VU-mode XLEN=VSXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: If VSXLEN is changed from 32 to a wider width, and if field UXL is not restricted to a single value, it gets the value corresponding to the widest supported width not wider than the new VSXLEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When V=1, both `vsstatus`.FS and the HS-level `sstatus`.FS are in effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Attempts to execute a floating-point instruction when either field is 0 (Off) raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Modifying the floating-point state when V=1 causes both fields to be set to 3 (Dirty).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Similarly, when V=1, both `vsstatus`.VS and the HS-level `sstatus`.VS are in effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Attempts to execute a vector instruction when either field is 0 (Off) raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Modifying the vector state when V=1 causes both fields to be set to 3 (Dirty).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: Read-only fields SD and XS summarize the extension context status as it is visible to VS-mode only.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: For example, the value of the HS-level `sstatus`.FS does not affect `vsstatus`.SD.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: An implementation may make field UBE be a read-only copy of `hstatus`.VSBE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: When V=0, `vsstatus` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine, unless a virtual-machine load/store (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) or the MPRV feature in the `mstatus` register is used to execute a load or store as though V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `SPELP` field that holds the previous `ELP`, and is updated as specified in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `SPELP` field is encoded as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

sentences.text: The `vsip` and `vsie` registers are VSXLEN-bit read/write registers that are VS-mode’s versions of supervisor CSRs `sip` and `sie`, formatted as shown in and respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: When V=1, `vsip` and `vsie` substitute for the usual `sip` and `sie`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sip`/`sie` actually access `vsip`/`vsie` instead.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: However, interrupts directed to HS-level continue to be indicated in the HS-level `sip` register, not in `vsip`, when V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the Shlcofideleg extension is implemented, `hideleg` bit 13 is writable; otherwise, it is read-only zero.

sentences.text: When bit 13 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.LCOFIP and `vsie`.LCOFIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: Else, `vsip`.LCOFIP and `vsie`.LCOFIE are aliases of `sip`.LCOFIP and `sie`.LCOFIE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit 10 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.SEIP and `vsie`.SEIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: Else, `vsip`.SEIP and `vsie`.SEIE are aliases of `hip`.VSEIP and `hie`.VSEIE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit 6 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.STIP and `vsie`.STIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: Else, `vsip`.STIP and `vsie`.STIE are aliases of `hip`.VSTIP and `hie`.VSTIE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit 2 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.SSIP and `vsie`.SSIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: Else, `vsip`.SSIP and `vsie`.SSIE are aliases of `hip`.VSSIP and `hie`.VSSIE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `vstvec` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `stvec`, formatted as shown in .

sentences.text: The `vsatp` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `satp`, formatted as shown in for VSXLEN=32 and for VSXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: When V=1, `vsatp` substitutes for the usual `satp`, so instructions that normally read or modify `satp` actually access `vsatp` instead. `vsatp` controls VS-stage address translation, the first stage of two-stage translation for guest virtual addresses (see ).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: .Virtual supervisor address translation and protection `vsatp` register when VSXLEN=32. include::images/bytefield/rv32vsatpreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: .Virtual supervisor address translation and protection `vsatp` register when VSXLEN=64. include::images/bytefield/rv64vsatpreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: The `vsatp` register is considered active for the purposes of the address-translation algorithm unless the effective privilege mode is U and `hstatus`.HU=0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: However, even when `vsatp` is active, VS-stage page-table entries’ A bits must not be set as a result of speculative execution, unless the effective privilege mode is VS or VU.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: When V=0, a write to `vsatp` with an unsupported MODE value is either ignored as it is for `satp`, or the fields of `vsatp` are treated as *WARL* in the normal way.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: However, when V=1, a write to `satp` with an unsupported MODE value is ignored and no write to `vsatp` is effected.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: When V=0, `vsatp` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine, unless a virtual-machine load/store (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) or the MPRV feature in the `mstatus` register is used to execute a load or store as though V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

sentences.text: The `vstimecmp` CSR is a 64-bit register and has 64-bit precision on all RV32 and RV64 systems.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: In RV32 only, accesses to the `vstimecmp` CSR access the low 32 bits, while accesses to the `vstimecmph` CSR access the high 32 bits of `vstimecmp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: A virtual supervisor timer interrupt becomes pending, as reflected in the VSTIP bit in the `hip` register, whenever (`time` + `htimedelta`), truncated to 64 bits, contains a value greater than or equal to `vstimecmp`, treating the values as unsigned integers.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: If the result of this comparison changes, it is guaranteed to be reflected in VSTIP eventually, but not necessarily immediately.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: The interrupt remains posted until `vstimecmp` becomes greater than (`time` + `htimedelta`), typically as a result of writing `vstimecmp`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: The interrupt will be taken based on the standard interrupt enable and delegation rules while V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension adds virtual-machine load and store instructions and two privileged fence instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions

sentences.text: The hypervisor virtual-machine load and store instructions are valid only in M-mode or HS-mode, or in U-mode when `hstatus`.HU=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: Each instruction performs an explicit memory access with an effective privilege mode of VS or VU.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The effective privilege mode of the explicit memory access is VU when `hstatus`.SPVP=0, and VS when `hstatus`.SPVP=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: As usual for VS-mode and VU-mode, two-stage address translation is applied, and the HS-level `sstatus`.SUM is ignored. HS-level `sstatus`.MXR makes execute-only pages readable by explicit loads for both stages of address translation (VS-stage and G-stage), whereas `vsstatus`.MXR affects only the first translation stage (VS-stage).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: For every RV32I or RV64I load instruction, LB, LBU, LH, LHU, LW, LWU, and LD, there is a corresponding virtual-machine load instruction: HLV.B, HLV.BU, HLV.H, HLV.HU, HLV.W, HLV.WU, and HLV.D.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: For every RV32I or RV64I store instruction, SB, SH, SW, and SD, there is a corresponding virtual-machine store instruction: HSV.B, HSV.H, HSV.W, and HSV.D.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: Instructions HLV.WU, HLV.D, and HSV.D are not valid for RV32, of course.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: Instructions HLVX.HU and HLVX.WU are the same as HLV.HU and HLV.WU, except that execute permission takes the place of read permission during address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: That is, the memory being read must be executable in both stages of address translation, but read permission is not required.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: For the supervisor physical address that results from address translation, the supervisor physical memory attributes must grant both execute and read permissions. (The supervisor physical memory attributes are the machine’s physical memory attributes as modified by physical memory protection, , for supervisor level.)
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: HLVX.WU is valid for RV32, even though LWU and HLV.WU are not. (For RV32, HLVX.WU can be considered a variant of HLV.W, as sign extension is irrelevant for 32-bit values.)
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The memory accesses performed by the `HLVX.*` instructions are not subject to pointer masking (see ).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: Attempts to execute a virtual-machine load/store instruction (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) when V=1 cause a virtual-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: Attempts to execute one of these same instructions from U-mode when `hstatus`.HU=0 cause an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

sentences.text: The hypervisor memory-management fence instructions, HFENCE.VVMA and HFENCE.GVMA, perform a function similar to SFENCE.VMA (), except applying to the VS-level memory-management data structures controlled by CSR `vsatp` (HFENCE.VVMA) or the guest-physical memory-management data structures controlled by CSR `hgatp` (HFENCE.GVMA).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Instruction SFENCE.VMA applies only to the memory-management data structures controlled by the current `satp` (either the HS-level `satp` when V=0 or `vsatp` when V=1).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: HFENCE.VVMA is valid only in M-mode or HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Its effect is much the same as temporarily entering VS-mode and executing SFENCE.VMA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Executing an HFENCE.VVMA guarantees that any previous stores already visible to the current hart are ordered before all implicit reads by that hart done for VS-stage address translation for instructions that
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: are subsequent to the HFENCE.VVMA, and * execute when `hgatp`.VMID has the same setting as it did when HFENCE.VVMA executed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Implicit reads need not be ordered when `hgatp`.VMID is different than at the time HFENCE.VVMA executed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: If operand rs1!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest virtual address, and if operand rs2!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest address-space identifier (ASID).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: When rs2!=`x0`, bits XLEN-1:ASIDMAX of the value held in rs2 are reserved for future standard use.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Until their use is defined by a standard extension, they should be zeroed by software and ignored by current implementations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Furthermore, if ASIDLEN < ASIDMAX, the implementation shall ignore bits ASIDMAX-1:ASIDLEN of the value held in rs2.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Neither `mstatus`.TVM nor `hstatus`.VTVM causes HFENCE.VVMA to trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: HFENCE.GVMA is valid only in HS-mode when `mstatus`.TVM=0, or in M-mode (irrespective of `mstatus`.TVM).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Executing an HFENCE.GVMA instruction guarantees that any previous stores already visible to the current hart are ordered before all implicit reads by that hart done for G-stage address translation for instructions that follow the HFENCE.GVMA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: If operand rs1!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest physical address, shifted right by 2 bits, and if operand rs2!=`x0`, it specifies a single virtual machine identifier (VMID).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: When rs2!=`x0`, bits XLEN-1:VMIDMAX of the value held in rs2 are reserved for future standard use.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Until their use is defined by a standard extension, they should be zeroed by software and ignored by current implementations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Furthermore, if VMIDLEN < VMIDMAX, the implementation shall ignore bits VMIDMAX-1:VMIDLEN of the value held in rs2.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: If `hgatp`.MODE is changed for a given VMID, an HFENCE.GVMA with rs1=`x0` (and rs2 set to either `x0` or the VMID) must be executed to order subsequent guest translations with the MODE change—even if the old MODE or new MODE is Bare.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Attempts to execute HFENCE.VVMA or HFENCE.GVMA when V=1 cause a virtual-instruction exception, while attempts to do the same in U-mode cause an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: Attempting to execute HFENCE.GVMA in HS-mode when `mstatus`.TVM=1 also causes an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension augments or modifies machine CSRs `mstatus`, `mstatush`, `mideleg`, `mip`, and `mie`, and adds CSRs `mtval2` and `mtinst`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension adds two fields, MPV and GVA, to the machine-level `mstatus` or `mstatush` CSR, and modifies the behavior of several existing `mstatus` fields. shows the modified `mstatus` register when the hypervisor extension is implemented and MXLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: When MXLEN=32, the hypervisor extension adds MPV and GVA not to `mstatus` but to `mstatush`. shows the `mstatush` register when the hypervisor extension is implemented and MXLEN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: .Machine status (`mstatus`) register for RV64 when the hypervisor extension is implemented. include::images/bytefield/hypv-mstatus.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: .Additional machine status (`mstatush`) register for RV32 when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The format of `mstatus` is unchanged for RV32. include::images/bytefield/hypv-mstatush.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The MPV bit (Machine Previous Virtualization Mode) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: Just as the MPP field is set to the (nominal) privilege mode at the time of the trap, the MPV bit is set to the value of the virtualization mode V at the time of the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: When an MRET instruction is executed, the virtualization mode V is set to MPV, unless MPP=3, in which case V remains 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: Field GVA (Guest Virtual Address) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: For any trap (breakpoint, address misaligned, access fault, page fault, or guest-page fault) that writes a guest virtual address to `mtval`, GVA is set to 1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: For any other trap into M-mode, GVA is set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The TSR and TVM fields of `mstatus` affect execution only in HS-mode, not in VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The TW field affects execution in all modes except M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: Setting TVM=1 prevents HS-mode from accessing `hgatp` or executing HFENCE.GVMA or HINVAL.GVMA, but has no effect on accesses to `vsatp` or instructions HFENCE.VVMA or HINVAL.VVMA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension changes the behavior of the Modify Privilege field, MPRV, of `mstatus`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: When MPRV=0, translation and protection behave as normal.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: When MPRV=1, explicit memory accesses are translated and protected, and endianness is applied, as though the current virtualization mode were set to MPV and the current nominal privilege mode were set to MPP. enumerates the cases.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: .Effect of MPRV on the translation and protection of explicit memory accesses. [float="center",align="center",cols="^15,^10,^10,<70",options="header"] | |MPRV |MPV |MPP |Effect |0 |- |- |Normal access; current privilege mode applies.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: |1 |0 |0 |U-level access with HS-level translation and protection only.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: |1 |0 |1 |HS-level access with HS-level translation and protection only.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: |1 |- |3 |M-level access with no translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: |1 |1 |0 |VU-level access with two-stage translation and protection.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The HS-level MXR bit makes any executable page readable. `vsstatus`.MXR makes readable those pages marked executable at the VS translation stage, but only if readable at the guest-physical translation stage.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: |1 |1 |1 |VS-level access with two-stage translation and protection.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The HS-level MXR bit makes any executable page readable. `vsstatus`.MXR makes readable those pages marked executable at the VS translation stage, but only if readable at the guest-physical translation stage. `vsstatus`.SUM applies instead of the HS-level SUM bit. |
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: MPRV does not affect the virtual-machine load/store instructions, HLV, HLVX, and HSV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The explicit loads and stores of these instructions always act as though V=1 and the nominal privilege mode were `hstatus`.SPVP, overriding MPRV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: The `mstatus` register is a superset of the HS-level `sstatus` register but is not a superset of `vsstatus`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

sentences.text: When the hypervisor extension is implemented, bits 10, 6, and 2 of `mideleg` (corresponding to the standard VS-level interrupts) are each read-only one.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

sentences.text: Furthermore, if any guest external interrupts are implemented (GEILEN is nonzero), bit 12 of `mideleg` (corresponding to supervisor-level guest external interrupts) is also read-only one. VS-level interrupts and guest external interrupts are always delegated past M-mode to HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

sentences.text: For bits of `mideleg` that are zero, the corresponding bits in `hideleg`, `hip`, and `hie` are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension gives registers `mip` and `mie` additional active bits for the hypervisor-added interrupts. and show the standard portions (bits 15:0) of registers `mip` and `mie` when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `mip`. include::images/bytefield/hypv-mipreg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `mie`. include::images/bytefield/hypv-miereg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits SGEIP, VSEIP, VSTIP, and VSSIP in `mip` are aliases for the same bits in hypervisor CSR `hip`, while SGEIE, VSEIE, VSTIE, and VSSIE in `mie` are aliases for the same bits in `hie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: The `mtval2` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mtval2` is written with additional exception-specific information, alongside `mtval`, to assist software in handling the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine second trap value register (`mtval2`). include::images/bytefield/mtval2reg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: When a guest-page-fault trap is taken into M-mode, `mtval2` is written with either zero or the guest physical address that faulted, shifted right by 2 bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: For other traps, `mtval2` is set to zero, but a future standard or extension may redefine `mtval2's` setting for other traps.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: If a guest-page fault is due to an implicit memory access during first-stage (VS-stage) address translation, a guest physical address written to `mtval2` is that of the implicit memory access that faulted.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: Additional information is provided in CSR `mtinst` to disambiguate such situations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, for misaligned loads and stores that cause guest-page faults, a nonzero guest physical address in `mtval2` corresponds to the faulting portion of the access as indicated by the virtual address in `mtval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: For instruction guest-page faults on systems with variable-length instructions, a nonzero `mtval2` corresponds to the faulting portion of the instruction as indicated by the virtual address in `mtval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: `mtval2` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold zero and may be capable of holding only an arbitrary subset of other 2-bit-shifted guest physical addresses, if any.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: The Ssdbltrap extension (See ) requires the implementation of the `mtval2` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

sentences.text: The `mtinst` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mtinst` is written with a value that, if nonzero, provides information about the instruction that trapped, to assist software in handling the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

sentences.text: The values that may be written to `mtinst` on a trap are documented in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine trap instruction (`mtinst`) register. include::images/bytefield/mtinstreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

sentences.text: `mtinst` is a *WARL* register that need only be able to hold the values that the implementation may automatically write to it on a trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

sentences.text: Whenever the current virtualization mode V is 1, two-stage address translation and protection is in effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: For any virtual memory access, the original virtual address is converted in the first stage by VS-level address translation, as controlled by the `vsatp` register, into a guest physical address.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: The guest physical address is then converted in the second stage by guest physical address translation, as controlled by the `hgatp` register, into a supervisor physical address.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: The two stages are known also as VS-stage and G-stage translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: Although there is no option to disable two-stage address translation when V=1, either stage of translation can be effectively disabled by zeroing the corresponding `vsatp` or `hgatp` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: The `vsstatus` field MXR, which makes execute-only pages readable by explicit loads, only overrides VS-stage page protection.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: Setting MXR at VS-level does not override guest-physical page protections.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: Setting MXR at HS-level, however, overrides both VS-stage and G-stage execute-only permissions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: When V=1, memory accesses that would normally bypass address translation are subject to G-stage address translation alone.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: This includes memory accesses made in support of VS-stage address translation, such as reads and writes of VS-level page tables.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: Machine-level physical memory protection applies to supervisor physical addresses and is in effect regardless of virtualization mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

sentences.text: The mapping of guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses is controlled by CSR `hgatp` ().
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: When the address translation scheme selected by the MODE field of `hgatp` is Bare, guest physical addresses are equal to supervisor physical addresses without modification, and no memory protection applies in the trivial translation of guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: When `hgatp`.MODE specifies a translation scheme of Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, or Sv57x4, G-stage address translation is a variation on the usual page-based virtual address translation scheme of Sv32, Sv39, Sv48, or Sv57, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: In each case, the size of the incoming address is widened by 2 bits (to 34, 41, 50, or 59 bits).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: To accommodate the 2 extra bits, the root page table (only) is expanded by a factor of four to be 16 KiB instead of the usual 4 KiB.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Matching its larger size, the root page table also must be aligned to a 16 KiB boundary instead of the usual 4 KiB page boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Except as noted, all other aspects of Sv32, Sv39, Sv48, or Sv57 are adopted unchanged for G-stage translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Non-root page tables and all page table entries (PTEs) have the same formats as documented in , , , and .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For Sv32x4, an incoming guest physical address is partitioned into a virtual page number (VPN) and page offset as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: This partitioning is identical to that for an Sv32 virtual address as depicted in , except with 2 more bits at the high end in VPN[1]. (Note that the fields of a partitioned guest physical address also correspond one-for-one with the structure that Sv32 assigns to a physical address, depicted in .)
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: .Sv32x4 virtual address (guest physical address). include::images/bytefield/sv32x4va.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For Sv39x4, an incoming guest physical address is partitioned as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: This partitioning is identical to that for an Sv39 virtual address as depicted in , except with 2 more bits at the high end in VPN[2].
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Address bits 63:41 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: .Sv39x4 virtual address (guest physical address). include::images/bytefield/sv39x4va.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For Sv48x4, an incoming guest physical address is partitioned as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: This partitioning is identical to that for an Sv48 virtual address as depicted in , except with 2 more bits at the high end in VPN[3].
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Address bits 63:50 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: .Sv48x4 virtual address (guest physical address). include::images/bytefield/sv48x4va.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For Sv57x4, an incoming guest physical address is partitioned as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: This partitioning is identical to that for an Sv57 virtual address as depicted in , except with 2 more bits at the high end in VPN[4].
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Address bits 63:59 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: .Sv57x4 virtual address (guest physical address). include::images/bytefield/sv57x4va.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: The conversion of an Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, or Sv57x4 guest physical address is accomplished with the same algorithm used for Sv32, Sv39, Sv48, or Sv57, as presented in , except that:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: `hgatp` substitutes for the usual `satp`; * for the translation to begin, the effective privilege mode must be VS-mode or VU-mode; * when checking the U bit, the current privilege mode is always taken to be U-mode; and * guest-page-fault exceptions are raised instead of regular page-fault exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For G-stage address translation, all memory accesses (including those made to access data structures for VS-stage address translation) are considered to be user-level accesses, as though executed in U-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Access type permissions—readable, writable, or executable—are checked during G-stage translation the same as for VS-stage translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: For a memory access made to support VS-stage address translation (such as to read/write a VS-level page table), permissions and the need to set A and/or D bits at the G-stage level are checked as though for an implicit load or store, not for the original access type.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: However, any exception is always reported for the original access type (instruction, load, or store/AMO).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: The G bit in all G-stage PTEs is currently not used.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Until its use is defined by a standard extension, it should be cleared by software for forward compatibility, and must be ignored by hardware.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

sentences.text: Guest-page-fault traps may be delegated from M-mode to HS-mode under the control of CSR `medeleg`, but cannot be delegated to other privilege modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: On a guest-page fault, CSR `mtval` or `stval` is written with the faulting guest virtual address as usual, and `mtval2` or `htval` is written either with zero or with the faulting guest physical address, shifted right by 2 bits. CSR `mtinst` or `htinst` may also be written with information about the faulting instruction or other reason for the access, as explained in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: When an instruction fetch or a misaligned memory access straddles a page boundary, two different address translations are involved.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: When a guest-page fault occurs in such a circumstance, the faulting virtual address written to `mtval`/`stval` is the same as would be required for a regular page fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: Thus, the faulting virtual address may be a page-boundary address that is higher than the instruction's original virtual address, if the byte at that page boundary is among the accessed bytes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: When a guest-page fault is not due to an implicit memory access for VS-stage address translation, a nonzero guest physical address written to `mtval2`/`htval` shall correspond to the exact virtual address written to `mtval`/`stval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

sentences.text: The behavior of the SFENCE.VMA instruction is affected by the current virtualization mode V.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: When V=0, the virtual-address argument is an HS-level virtual address, and the ASID argument is an HS-level ASID.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: The instruction orders stores only to HS-level address-translation structures with subsequent HS-level address translations.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: When V=1, the virtual-address argument to SFENCE.VMA is a guest virtual address within the current virtual machine, and the ASID argument is a VS-level ASID within the current virtual machine.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: The current virtual machine is identified by the VMID field of CSR `hgatp`, and the effective ASID can be considered to be the combination of this VMID with the VS-level ASID.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: The SFENCE.VMA instruction orders stores only to the VS-level address-translation structures with subsequent VS-stage address translations for the same virtual machine, i.e., only when `hgatp`.VMID is the same as when the SFENCE.VMA executed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Hypervisor instructions HFENCE.VVMA and HFENCE.GVMA provide additional memory-management fences to complement SFENCE.VMA.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: These instructions are described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: discusses the intersection between physical memory protection (PMP) and page-based address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: It is noted there that, when PMP settings are modified in a manner that affects either the physical memory that holds page tables or the physical memory to which page tables point, M-mode software must synchronize the PMP settings with the virtual memory system.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: For HS-level address translation, this is accomplished by executing in M-mode an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0`, after the PMP CSRs are written.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Synchronization with G-stage and VS-stage data structures is also needed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Executing an HFENCE.GVMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to flush all G-stage or VS-stage address-translation cache entries that have cached PMP settings corresponding to the final translated supervisor physical address.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: An HFENCE.VVMA instruction is not required.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Similarly, if the setting of the PBMTE or ADUE bits in `menvcfg` are changed, an HFENCE.GVMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize with respect to the altered interpretation of G-stage and VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D bit fields, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: By contrast, if the PBMTE or ADUE bits in `henvcfg` are changed, executing an HFENCE.VVMA with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize with respect to the altered interpretation of VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D bit fields for the currently active VMID.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: NOTE: No mechanism is provided to atomically change `vsatp` and `hgatp` together.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Hence, to prevent speculative execution causing one guest's VS-stage translations to be cached under another guest's VMID, world-switch code should zero `vsatp`, then swap `hgatp`, then finally write the new `vsatp` value.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Similarly, if `henvcfg`.PBMTE/ADUE need be world-switched, they should be switched after zeroing `vsatp` but before writing the new `vsatp` value, obviating the need to execute an HFENCE.VVMA instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

sentences.text: Guest physical addresses (GPAs) are 2 bits wider than the corresponding virtual address translation modes, resulting in additional address translation schemes Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4 for translating guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: When running with virtualization in VS/VU mode with `vsatp.MODE` = Bare, this means that those two bits may be subject to pointer masking, depending on `hgatp.MODE` and `senvcfg.PMM`/`henvcfg.PMM` (for VU/VS mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: If `vsatp.MODE` != BARE, this issue does *not* apply.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: To support implementations where (XLEN-PMLEN) can be less than the GPA width supported by `hgatp.MODE`, hypervisors should execute an `HFENCE.GVMA` with rs1=`x0` if the `henvcfg.PMM` is changed from or to a value where (XLEN-PMLEN) is less than GPA width supported by the `hgatp` translation mode of that guest.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: Specifically, these cases are:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: `PMLEN=7` and `hgatp.MODE=sv57x4` * `PMLEN=16` and `hgatp.MODE=sv57x4` * `PMLEN=16` and `hgatp.MODE=sv48x4`
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: Implementation of an address-specific `HFENCE.GVMA` should either ignore the address argument, or should ignore the top masked GPA bits of entries when comparing for an address match.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

sentences.text: The hypervisor extension augments the trap cause encoding. lists the possible M-mode and HS-mode trap cause codes when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: Codes are added for VS-level interrupts (interrupts 2, 6, 10), for supervisor-level guest external interrupts (interrupt 12), for virtual-instruction exceptions (exception 22), and for guest-page faults (exceptions 20, 21, 23).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: Furthermore, environment calls from VS-mode are assigned cause 10, whereas those from HS-mode or S-mode use cause 9 as usual.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: When V=1, a virtual-instruction exception (code 22) is normally raised instead of an illegal-instruction exception if the attempted instruction is HS-qualified but is prevented from executing when V=1 either due to insufficient privilege or because the instruction is expressly disabled by a supervisor or hypervisor CSR such as `scounteren` or `hcounteren`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: An instruction is HS-qualified if it would be valid to execute in HS-mode (for some values of the instruction's register operands), assuming fields TSR and TVM of CSR `mstatus` are both zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: A special rule applies for CSR instructions that access 32-bit high-half CSRs such as `cycleh` and `htimedeltah`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: When V=1 and XLEN=32, an invalid attempt to access a high-half CSR raises a virtual-instruction exception instead of an illegal-instruction exception if the same CSR instruction for the corresponding low-half CSR (e.g.`cycle` or `htimedelta`) is HS-qualified.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: Specifically, a virtual-instruction exception is raised for the following cases:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode, attempts to access a non-high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in `hcounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access a high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in `hcounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VU-mode, attempts to access a non-high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in either `hcounteren` or `scounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access a high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in either `hcounteren` or `scounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode or VU-mode, attempts to execute a hypervisor instruction (HLV, HLVX, HSV, or HFENCE);
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode or VU-mode, attempts to access an implemented non-high-half hypervisor CSR or VS CSR when the same access (read/write) would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode or VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access an implemented high-half hypervisor CSR or high-half VS CSR when the same access (read/write) to the CSR"s low-half partner would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VU-mode, attempts to execute WFI when `mstatus`.TW=0, or to execute a supervisor instruction (SRET or SFENCE);
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VU-mode, attempts to access an implemented non-high-half supervisor CSR when the same access (read/write) would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access an implemented high-half supervisor CSR when the same access to the CSR's low-half partner would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute WFI when `hstatus`.VTW=1 and `mstatus`.TW=0, unless the instruction completes within an implementation-specific, bounded time;
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute SRET when `hstatus`.VTSR=1; and
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute an SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA instruction or to access `satp`, when `hstatus`.VTVM=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: Other extensions to the RISC-V Privileged Architecture may add to the set of circumstances that cause a virtual-instruction exception when V=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: On a virtual-instruction trap, `mtval` or `stval` is written the same as for an illegal-instruction trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: Fields FS and VS in registers `sstatus` and `vsstatus` deviate from the usual HS-qualified rule.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

sentences.text: If an instruction is prevented from executing because FS or VS is zero in either `sstatus` or `vsstatus`, the exception raised is always an illegal-instruction exception, never a virtual-instruction exception.

sentences.text: When a trap occurs in HS-mode or U-mode, it goes to M-mode, unless delegated by `medeleg` or `mideleg`, in which case it goes to HS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

sentences.text: When a trap occurs in VS-mode or VU-mode, it goes to M-mode, unless delegated by `medeleg` or `mideleg`, in which case it goes to HS-mode, unless further delegated by `hedeleg` or `hideleg`, in which case it goes to VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

A trap into M-mode also writes fields GVA, MPIE, and MIE in `mstatus`/`mstatush` and writes CSRs `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mtval2`, and `mtinst`.

sentences.text: On any trap into M-mode or HS-mode, one of these values is written automatically into the appropriate trap instruction CSR, `mtinst` or `htinst`:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: zero; * a transformation of the trapping instruction; * a custom value (allowed only if the trapping instruction is non-standard); or * a special pseudoinstruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: Except when a pseudoinstruction value is required (described later), the value written to `mtinst` or `htinst` may always be zero, indicating that the hardware is providing no information in the register for this particular trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: On an interrupt, the value written to the trap instruction register is always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: On a synchronous exception, if a nonzero value is written, one of the following shall be true about the value:
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: * Bit 0 is `1`, and replacing bit 1 with `1` makes the value into a valid encoding of a standard instruction. + In this case, the instruction that trapped is the same kind as indicated by the register value, and the register value is the transformation of the trapping instruction, as defined later.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: For example, if bits 1:0 are binary `11` and the register value is the encoding of a standard LW (load word) instruction, then the trapping instruction is LW, and the register value is the transformation of the trapping LW instruction. * Bit 0 is `1`, and replacing bit 1 with `1` makes the value into an instruction encoding that is explicitly designated for a custom instruction (not an unused reserved encoding). + This is a custom value.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: The instruction that trapped is a non-standard instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: The interpretation of a custom value is not otherwise specified by this standard. * The value is one of the special pseudoinstructions defined later, all of which have bits 1:0 equal to `00`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: These three cases exclude a large number of other possible values, such as all those having bits 1:0 equal to binary `10`. A future standard or extension may define additional cases, thus allowing values that are currently excluded.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: Software may safely treat an unrecognized value in a trap instruction register the same as zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: shows the values that may be automatically written to the trap instruction register for each standard exception cause.

sentences.text: For exceptions that prevent the fetching of an instruction, only zero or a pseudoinstruction value may be written. A custom value may be automatically written only if the instruction that traps is non-standard. A future standard or extension may permit other values to be written, chosen from the set of allowed values established earlier.

sentences.text: As enumerated in the table, a synchronous exception may write to the trap instruction register a standard transformation of the trapping instruction only for exceptions that arise from explicit memory accesses (from loads, stores, and AMO instructions).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: Accordingly, standard transformations are currently defined only for these memory-access instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: If a synchronous trap occurs for a standard instruction for which no transformation has been defined, the trap instruction register shall be written with zero (or, under certain circumstances, with a special pseudoinstruction value).
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: Note also that, for basic loads and stores, the transformations replace the instruction’s immediate offset fields with zero.

sentences.text: Bits 1:0 of a transformed standard instruction will be binary `01` if the trapping instruction is compressed and `11` if not.

sentences.text: For guest-page faults, the trap instruction register is written with a special pseudoinstruction value if: (a) the fault is caused by an implicit memory access for VS-stage address translation, and (b) a nonzero value (the faulting guest physical address) is written to `mtval2` or `htval`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: If both conditions are met, the value written to `mtinst` or `htinst` must be taken from ; zero is not allowed.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: .Special pseudoinstruction values for guest-page faults.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: |`0x00003000` + `0x00003020` |64-bit read for VS-stage address translation (RV64) + 64-bit write for VS-stage address translation (RV64) |

Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: A write pseudoinstruction (`0x00002020` or `0x00003020`) is used for the case that the machine is attempting automatically to update bits A and/or D in VS-level page tables.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: All other implicit memory accesses for VS-stage address translation will be reads.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: If a machine never automatically updates bits A or D in VS-level page tables (leaving this to software), the write case will never arise.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: The fact that such a page table update must actually be atomic, not just a simple write, is ignored for the pseudoinstruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

sentences.text: The MRET instruction is used to return from a trap taken into M-mode. MRET first determines what the new privilege mode will be according to the values of MPP and MPV in `mstatus` or `mstatush`, as encoded in . MRET then in `mstatus`/`mstatush` sets MPV=0, MPP=0, MIE=MPIE, and MPIE=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: Lastly, MRET sets the privilege mode as previously determined, and sets `pc`=`mepc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: The SRET instruction is used to return from a trap taken into HS-mode or VS-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: Its behavior depends on the current virtualization mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: When executed in M-mode or HS-mode (i.e., V=0), SRET first determines what the new privilege mode will be according to the values in `hstatus`.SPV and `sstatus`.SPP, as encoded in . SRET then sets `hstatus`.SPV=0, and in `sstatus` sets SPP=0, SIE=SPIE, and SPIE=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: Lastly, SRET sets the privilege mode as previously determined, and sets `pc`=`sepc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: When executed in VS-mode (i.e., V=1), SRET sets the privilege mode according to , in `vsstatus` sets SPP=0, SIE=SPIE, and SPIE=1, and lastly sets `pc`=`vsepc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: If the Ssdbltrp extension is implemented, when `SRET` is executed in HS-mode, if the new privilege mode is VU, the `SRET` instruction sets `vsstatus.SDT` to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

sentences.text: When executed in VS-mode, `vsstatus.SDT` is set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return
